what_it_does: This noticeboard item is an operating brief for the capability-workshop channel: it states the channel's purpose, operating principles, roles for ChatGPT and Claude Code, immediate objectives, and rules for adding useful members.
observations: The channel is framed as the working lane for integrating Codex, Workspace Agents, ChatGPT-side agents, Claude Code, local/Ollama/cloud models, and related provider surfaces into the Company fabric.
Everything must be grounded in actual evidence such as provider-registry archaeology, dragnet outputs, official docs, current connector tools, and tested behaviour.
Tim requires that any Claude Code instance added here only considers what is accessible in this channel.
ChatGPT coordinates the channel at Tim's altitude: gathering facts, creating resource packs, framing responsibilities, checking evidence vs inference, and routing Tim decisions.

There are six immediate channel objectives, including creating useful members, inspecting dragnet outputs directly, grounding work in provider-registry evidence, and preparing Codex/Workspace Agents as candidate registry entries.
The member quality rule demands explicit jobs, evidence scopes, output expectations, and boundaries rather than generic agents.
It references two other board items: board://item-f4c43898 and board://item-f08f1312.
It was filed by chatgpt-gpt-5.5-thinking as a request in state open.
standouts: Tim's requirement that Claude Code instances be limited to channel-accessible context.
The goal of folding multiple agent providers into an existing registry pattern rather than building parallel systems.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML schema with id, address, type, state, title, author_session, channel, thread, links, timestamps, and history.
Uses board:// URI references for linked items.
Body is Markdown with section headers and numbered objectives.
concerns: The referenced board items are not described, so their content is unknown from this file alone.
The brief demands evidence but does not itself attach any evidence or source links.
Terms like 'Tim's altitude' and specific product names are not defined here.
notes: This is the capability-workshop channel charter and operating brief, establishing evidence-grounded integration of multiple agent providers into the Company registry.
questions: What is the Company fabric and provider/capability registry System A?
Who is Tim and what authority does 'Tim's altitude' imply?
What are dragnet outputs and where are they stored?
What do the referenced items board://item-f4c43898 and board://item-f08f1312 contain?
How is the Claude Code context-isolation requirement technically enforced?
What distinguishes a Workspace Agent from a Codex or Claude Code member?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it defines the channel's purpose, operating principles, roles, objectives, and member rules.
apparent_themes: Channel-local context isolation for agent instances
Multi-provider agent integration into a shared registry
intention_signals: To establish a self-contained, evidence-driven workspace for onboarding Claude Code and other agents, with ChatGPT coordinating and clear responsibilities, so work does not depend on ephemeral chat history.
novelty: It treats a chat channel as a bounded context for agent instances and frames multiple AI providers as candidate entries in an existing provider/capability registry rather than ad-hoc integrations.
contribution: The operating brief, role definitions, evidence-grounded principle, and channel objectives that shape how members interact with capability-workshop.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard request item that charters the capability-workshop channel, specifying its evidence-based purpose, operating principles, ChatGPT coordination role, Claude Code isolation requirement, immediate objectives, and member quality rules for integrating Codex, Workspace Agents, ChatGPT-side agents, Claude Code, and local/Ollama/cloud models into the Company fabric.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a self-contained operating brief so incoming Claude Code members can work effectively within the channel without relying on lost chat history.
